title: SoAmanda Winn Lee
wikitext:
[]: So...Amanda Winn Lee             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Sun Feb 03, 2013 10:29 pm I like her voice acting well enough, but has she always been this...eccentric? These are a few recent posts from her fan page on Facebook: Okay, so at an Evangelion panel, a guy asked me what Anno and Gainax had to say about me screwing up the movie. This was basically my response (more or less): Get a fucking life. (Crowd says "whoa...") No, seriously. It's just a fucking cartoon. it's not going to cure cancer or feed the hungry. It's JUST a cartoon. Look, I get how anime can be amazing. Artistically, it can be beautiful and evoke an emotional and visceral response, and that's fantastic. but then take that energy and DO something with it. Don't just sit there watching tv in your parent's basement and spend your energy bitching about cartoons. Go out and DO something. You feel bad for Shinji? Go become a big brother or big sister. You don't like the way the world is? Then go out and do something in your community. Don't just sit there spending all your energy on cartoons. And to answer your question, Gainax had final approval over every single frame of that movie so ask them.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Sun Feb 03, 2013 10:45 pm She has had rather bad experiences with Eva fandom (and a particularly terrible one), plus her work in the dub, along with that of the other voice actors, is often held as a target of shame and ridicule, not to mention that apparently some eva fans boycott or harass them because of their shitty dubbing, so i'm not surprised in the least that she replied with such an outburst, i think it's pretty understandable in fact. Plus i don't think you can really disagree with what she says on the first quote, do you? Aside from that it seems voice acting doesn't pay well and dub voice actors don't get much respect to say the least, combine that with the fact that the actors don't really see much of the benefits of their work (apparently the studio takes it all) and that she has a family to take care of and i think you can see why she was so angry in there, and even to all that you have to add the aforementioned fans who boycott/harass/make fun of her. I don't know, i don't see anything eccentric or disrespectful in those two quotes, just a generally nice woman who is in a stressful situation that seems to have gotten sick of answering unfriendly and inappropiate questions of entitled fans.

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Sun Feb 03, 2013 10:46 pm ...that guy who was mentioned in the first post? thatwasmewhoaskedthequestion

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Sun Feb 03, 2013 10:49 pm C.A.P. wrote: ...that guy who mentioned that thing in the first post? thatwasme

[]:             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Sun Feb 03, 2013 11:07 pm Dream wrote: Stuff

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Sun Feb 03, 2013 11:09 pm I don't think she was implying that, it was kind of an outburst so likely she didn't think of all the ramifications of what she was saying, just responding to something that was bothering her for some time. Also, i can't really see how you took it that way.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sun Feb 03, 2013 11:20 pm Goddamn it C.A.P. And we were the ones who invited her to this forum, for fucks sake.

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Sun Feb 03, 2013 11:29 pm Right now, I'm hesitant about saying what happened at the event, but right now, I'll say this... Hours after the panel was done, and before Amanda was autographing things for her autograph session (though, don't take my word on that, just know that I had the following with me), I bought the first Evangelion omnibus out of an impulse to buy some kind of manga over at that con. Sometime before I bought it, I was thinking about getting a few autographs from the dub crew--I ended just getting Grant's and Lee's--, and so, I was in the room where Amada was signing autographs for one specific hour. I don't remember what I exactly said to her, but when I went up to her to sign the back of the omnibus, I apologized to her and told her that I did knew people who did like whatever the dub ended up being; I think it was along the lines on how the English dub, to some people, is just as valid or just as good as the original show was. I told her it was no hard feelings, Amanda smiled and agreed it was nothing personal, signed the omnibus, while not in jest, like she was with the other guys in the room, signed the omnibus, I said my thanks, and walked off. Inside, she wrote: "To [my name]- Glad to meet a true fan! Glad Eva continues to bring you pleasure! Amanda Winn Lee" If anyone wants pics on that message, I'll be more than happy to share.

[]:             Quote Post by Ringworm128 » Sun Feb 03, 2013 11:45 pm On one hand I agree with her, but on the other I'm sick of the whole "people who sit around watching and talking about cartoons need to get a life" thing. Just because they made a Japanese cartoon into their hobby which they then put a lot of time and effort into doesn't mean they are any less of a person. If someone studied Birds day in day out until they knew everything about Birds and then put it all into a book people wouldn't go "I think he needs to stop focusing on Birds." They would go "wow, he really knows his stuff, he must be really dedicated." Same thing with sports, If someone dedicated their life to American football to the point where he won the SB and then went on to coach a SB winning team in his later years people would never say "he needs to get a girlfriend." Yet if someone dares to enjoy a show beyond just watching it without talking about it beyond some scenes they enjoyed they apparently need to "get a life".

[]:             Quote Post by Catamari » Sun Feb 03, 2013 11:54 pm Ringworm128 wrote: On one hand I agree with her, but on the other I'm sick of the whole "people who sit around watching and talking about cartoons need to get a life" thing. Just because they made a Japanese cartoon into their hobby which they then put a lot of time and effort into doesn't mean they are any less of a person. If someone studied Birds day in day out until they knew everything about Birds and then put it all into a book people wouldn't go "I think he needs to stop focusing on Birds." They would go "wow, he really knows his stuff, he must be really dedicated." Same thing with sports, If someone dedicated their life to American football to the point where he won the SB and then went on to coach a SB winning team in his later years people would never say "he needs to get a girlfriend." Yet if someone dares to enjoy a show beyond just watching it without talking about it beyond some scenes they enjoyed they apparently need to "get a life".

[]:             Quote Post by Ringworm128 » Mon Feb 04, 2013 12:04 am I fail to see how knowing what time of the day a Pigeon takes a dump helps better mankind.

[]:             Quote Post by Catamari » Mon Feb 04, 2013 12:08 am Ringworm128 wrote: I fail to see how knowing what time of the day a Pigeon takes a dump helps better mankind.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Mon Feb 04, 2013 12:09 am Ringworm128 wrote: I'm sick of the whole "people who sit around watching and talking about cartoons need to get a life" thing.

[]:             Quote Post by The Killer of Heroes » Mon Feb 04, 2013 12:13 am @Catamari: I'm not sure how you think following/playing a sport produces no benefits either. Sure maybe the knowing the sport itself doesn't do much, but its a convenient thing to base social gatherings around, actually playing it can provide exercise, learning how it functions can help develop analytical skills etc. Also people simply enjoy it. Fun/stress relief is extremely beneficial to society. That doesn't mean its for everyone of course, but I'd say it does help at a communal level at least.

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Mon Feb 04, 2013 12:39 am I should also mention that when I went up to Grant and got her autograph, before I went in for Amanda, she told me to not worry about it too much, once I mentioned I was that guy. She told me that they, the dub actors, would usually get one person in the audience or so that ask about the sort of question I asked whenever there was an Evangelion panel like the one I was in. For that panel, I just so happen to be that guy.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Feb 04, 2013 1:59 am Catamari wrote: something that produces absolutely no tangible benefits for society is considered "having a life" (football, specifically;

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Mon Feb 04, 2013 2:06 am Yeah of the three dub actors for the children, i think she's easily the one who does her character best.

[]:             Quote Post by SEELE-01 » Mon Feb 04, 2013 2:37 am Ringworm128 wrote: I fail to see how knowing what time of the day a Pigeon takes a dump helps better mankind.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Feb 04, 2013 1:57 pm Critical as I am of the dubs I can't fault Lee in the slightest -- some of the experiences she's had in this forum alone sucked mightily, and there's no excuse for that kind of shit when you're talking about a fucking cartoon. If someone's gonna bitch her out for screwing up the dub more than 15 years after the fact I don't blame her for being a bit testy in response. I also agree with the idea that she was reacting to bitchy fans and not to anime fans in general.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Feb 04, 2013 3:18 pm That first quote is actually really good. There are a lot of actors and VAs that get testy with fans and rightly so. It reminds me of a story about Alec Guinness; supposedly he met a little boy who'd seen Star Wars one hundred times, and Guinness urged him never to watch it ever again and do something else with his life.
